name: Episcopal Community
description_short: Episcopal Community FCU is a member-owned credit union serving the Episcopal Diocese of Los Angeles since 1994, offering savings, checking, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Episcopal Community Federal Credit Union has been serving members of the Episcopal Diocese of Los Angeles for nearly 30 years since its founding in 1994. As a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union, it operates under NCUA insurance and focuses on providing financial services tailored to its community. The institution positions itself as a values-driven alternative to traditional banks, emphasizing personalized financial guidance alongside competitive rates.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal loans, auto loans, church loans, savings accounts, and checking accounts. Members have access to online banking, mobile banking through iOS and Android apps, bill pay services, direct deposit, and Zelle money transfer integration. The institution maintains physical ATM locations and branch offices, with contact available at (213) 328-7756. All digital services are available 24/7, and the credit union emphasizes fast application review and funding processes.

Episcopal Community FCU distinguishes itself through its mission-driven approach to serving a specific faith community rather than the general public. The offering of specialized church loans indicates a niche focus on institutional lending to congregations. The credit union promotes fee-free services including direct deposit and bill pay, contrasting with many traditional banks. The emphasis on personalized financial guidance and serving a defined community member base represents a relationship-banking model rather than a transactional approach.

As a credit union, Episcopal Community FCU's primary limitation is membership eligibility—accounts are restricted to members of the Episcopal Diocese of Los Angeles and their families. While the website confirms competitive rates and terms, specific APRs, loan amounts, and account fee structures are not disclosed online, requiring direct member contact. The digital platform appears functional but basic compared to larger financial institutions, and loan product details (maximum amounts, eligibility requirements) are not publicly detailed.
services: Personal loans with competitive rates and terms
Online banking with transaction search and check images
Mobile banking app (iOS and Android) with 24/7 access
Bill pay with scheduled and recurring payments
Direct deposit with fund splitting capability
pros: Fee-free bill pay service with unlimited monthly payments and no monthly service charges
Free direct deposit with option to split deposits between multiple accounts
No fees for sending or receiving money via Zelle integration
24/7 access to accounts via mobile banking app (iOS and Android)
Specialized church loans available for congregational financing needs
Fast application review and funding processes promised
Mission-driven credit union focused on serving Episcopal faith community since 1994
Full-service online banking including eStatements, check images, and fund transfers
cons: Membership restricted to Episcopal Diocese of Los Angeles members and their families, limiting accessibility
Website lacks specific APR rates, loan amount limits, and detailed eligibility criteria for products
No mention of credit-building products, credit monitoring, or financial counseling services
Limited information about ATM network access or branch locations on main website
No details provided about account minimums, overdraft fees, or other account-related charges
best_for: Episcopal Diocese members seeking community-focused banking with faith-aligned values
Church organizations and congregations needing specialized institutional lending
Members prioritizing fee-free digital banking and bill pay services
Los Angeles area residents with existing ties to the Episcopal community
diagnosis: Episcopal Community FCU is best for members of the Episcopal Diocese of Los Angeles seeking a member-owned credit union with fee-free digital services and personalized lending. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to the Episcopal community, making this institution inaccessible to the general public regardless of creditworthiness or financial need.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Episcopal Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Los Angeles, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
